title: Cirsium polyphyllum
taxonomy: Cirsium polyphyllum is classified in the kingdom Plantae, and within the plant kingdom, it falls under the division Magnoliophyta. This plant is further classified in the class Magnoliopsida and order Asterales. Its family classification is Asteraceae, and its genus is Cirsium. The species Cirsium polyphyllum is a member of the Cirsium genus, which includes several species of thistle. At the species level, Cirsium polyphyllum is a distinct species within the Cirsium genus.
uses: Cirsium polyphyllum is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Asian cultures. The roots and flowers of the plant are said to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Traditional uses include treating fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. However, the efficacy and safety of these uses have not been extensively scientifically studied. More research is needed to confirm the medicinal properties of Cirsium polyphyllum.
benefits: Cirsium polyphyllum, also known as the many-leaved thistle, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. The plant contains various compounds that may have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. These properties are thought to contribute to the plant's potential in treating various health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions. Furthermore, Cirsium polyphyllum may also have antiseptic and anti-cancer properties, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. Overall, the plant is considered a valuable resource in traditional medicine, offering a range of potential health benefits.
properties:
Cirsium polyphyllum is a species of thistle native to western North America, and while specific research on its therapeutic properties is limited, some studies suggest that Cirsium species have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. 

Cirsium polyphyllum may offer potential benefits in wound healing, due to its antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, as well as its ability to promote tissue repair and reduce inflammation. Additionally, the plant's bioactive compounds may have anti-cancer properties and protect against oxidative stress. However, more research is needed to fully understand the therapeutic potential of Cirsium polyphyllum. Further studies are necessary to isolate and characterize the bioactive compounds present in the plant and to investigate its safety and efficacy for human use.
constituents: Cirsium polyphyllum contains sesquiterpene lactones, which are a type of active constituent found in various plants in the Asteraceae family. These compounds have been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The specific sesquiterpene lactones found in Cirsium polyphyllum include polyphyllin A and polyphyllin B. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential medicinal uses of Cirsium polyphyllum. intro: Cirsium polyphyllum, also known as many-headed thistle, is a perennial plant species within the sunflower family (Asteraceae) native to the mountainous regions of western North America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, offering potential health benefits in treating various conditions, including wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Cirsium polyphyllum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Cirsium polyphyllum include tinctures, infusions, and salves, which are often used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
